nvidiarobotics.text:
Who’s ready for this week’s Robotics Office Hours? 🙌

Join our experts tomorrow, Wed, Feb 18 at 11 AM PT to explore the latest in NVIDIA Isaac Lab and Newton, an open-source physics engine for robot development built on NVIDIA Warp and OpenUSD.

See practical demos in action, including robot locomotion and cloth folding. ➡️ https://nvda.ws/3ZDVlYW

RT by @NVIDIARobotics: How are developers using NVIDIA Cosmos Reason 2 for real-world physical AI?

On Feb. 19th, @milestonesys, @PlusAI_Inc, and @telitcinterion are showing how they leveraged it to:

1️⃣ Accelerate vision AI development with compliant, real-world video data

2️⃣ Enable real-time industrial automation and edge vision AI

Tune in to learn why Cosmos Reason 2 is becoming the go-to open reasoning VLM and how you can start building with it 👉 https://nvda.ws/4kI8PN0

Transforming robots from task-specific tools to intelligent collaborators is one of the toughest challenges in physical AI. 🦾

Join our researchers at #NVIDIAGTC to learn how they’re tackling this challenge with breakthroughs in sim-to-real, real-world learning, generalist embodied intelligence, and what’s next for robotics.

Add to schedule 🔗 https://nvda.ws/4c0HrHE

📅Tues, March 17 | 1:00 p.m PT

Speakers 🎤:
@DrJimFan 
@yukez 
Yashraj Narang
